info.title: AB Brain — Claude AI Integration
info.description:
AB Brain — Avi Bar's unified training, recovery, and race-prep platform. Schema for the Spartan Coach (Claude Project) and Claude Code routines.

## Base URL
https://ab-brain.up.railway.app/api

## Authentication
All endpoints require X-Api-Key header.

## Architecture (May 2026)

Two Claude layers consume this API and share the same voice + instructions:

1. **Claude Code routines** (autonomous, scheduled) — morning brief at 5am, evening review at 9pm. Sunday weekly scorecard, monthly physiology check, race-week pulse all fold into the morning brief conditionally. Routines write coaching_session records that surface on the home dashboard.

2. **Claude Project** (conversational) — image intake (food / RENPHO / Apple Watch / Fitbod screenshots), mid-day amend-day, fueling rehearsal capture, race debrief, ad-hoc questions.

Both layers also have **direct Apple Health MCP** access as a freshness fallback for same-day HRV/RHR/steps when AB Brain's HAE-fed data is stale (`is_stale = true`).

## Key endpoints (NEW since the March schema)

### Composite (call these instead of chaining 5 calls)
- **GET /health/insights/morning** — single morning bundle: readiness + alerts + active injuries + today's plan + yesterday's context + upcoming race + current training block + missing_subjective[]. The morning routine starts here.
- **GET /health/insights/race?race_id=** — race context: countdown, taper_phase (build/sharpen/taper/race_week/race_day), recommendation, recent fueling rehearsals, last-28d build summary.
- **GET /health/insights/weekly-review?week_of=** — 7-day retro: tss, distance, adherence_pct, polarization (with coverage_pct), sleep, HRV, nutrition, plan-vs-actual deltas.
- **GET /health/insights/polarization?weeks=N** — gray-zone drift detection over N weeks.
- **GET /health/insights/trends** — full aggregator (sleep / nutrition / training / body / vitals) with direction flags + targets + 90-day history. Now also returns ACWR, monotony, strain, polarization low/gray/high% on the training section.

### Race + periodization (NEW)
- **POST /races, GET /races, PUT /races/{id}** — first-class race calendar. A/B/C priority, distance, terrain, target_time, course_notes, fueling_plan, gear_list.
- **GET /races/upcoming** — next 5 scheduled with days_to_race.
- **POST /races/blocks, GET /races/blocks/current** — periodization blocks (offseason/base/build/peak/taper/race/transition/recovery) with thesis + target_race_id link.
- **POST /races/fueling, GET /races/fueling/list** — fueling rehearsal log (g_carb_per_hr, g_sodium_per_hr, fluid, products, gut/energy response 1-10). REFUSE race-week protocol if no rehearsal in last 28 days.

### Athlete profile (NEW)
- **GET /athlete/profile, POST /athlete/profile** — versioned physiology snapshot: lthr_bpm, max_hr_bpm, vo2_max, ftp_w, threshold_pace_sec_per_mi, sweat_rate_ml_per_hr, sodium_loss_mg_per_l, race_weight_lb. Same effective_from/effective_to pattern as athlete/zones.

### Targets (NEW)
- **GET /targets, PUT /targets/{metric}** — long-term goals (sleep_duration_min, protein_g, calories_kcal, weight_lb, weekly_z2_min, hrv_ms, etc.). Each returns current_value + on_track/below/above.

### daily_plans extensions (NEW columns)
- `intent_type` enum: recovery / aerobic_endurance / aerobic_durability / tempo / threshold / vo2max / race_specific / strength / mobility
- `phase` mirrors training_blocks.phase
- `linked_race_id`, `training_block_id`
- Plan macro targets (target_calories / target_protein_g / etc.) override the static MACRO_GOALS day-type table when set.

### daily_context extensions (NEW subjective columns)
- mood, motivation, soreness_overall (1-10), soreness_areas (jsonb), life_stress (1-10), illness_flag (none/onset/active/resolving), travel_status, bedtime_self_report
- POST is upsert-on-date with COALESCE per field — safe to call repeatedly during morning check-in.

## Hard rules (alerts that fire from /insights/morning, /today, /trends)

Check `alerts[]` on every response. Severity 'high' is non-negotiable.

- **Rule A — chronic_load** — 7d effort sum >50 for 5+ days OR ≥30% jump WoW → forced deload.
- **Rule B — density** — **2+** consecutive days at effort ≥7 → forced rest. (Tightened from 3.)
- **Rule C — rest-day underfueling** — yesterday rest-day protein <130g → recovery at risk.
- **Rule E — TSB crash** — TSB <-80 → forced rest; -30 to -80 → medium hold/reduce.
- **Rule F — sleep deprivation** — <5h single night = drop one effort tier; <5h two nights = halve intensity.
- **Active injury severity ≥3** → modify session per injury's modifications field. (Severity 3 IS the gate.)
- **Active illness flag** → rest until resolving + 3 days aerobic-only.
- **ACWR >1.5** → spike injury window; flat or reduced volume.

## Athlete-specific operating constraints

- **Hydration band:** 1.0L floor, 2.0L ceiling, 1.5L mid-target. Never set target_hydration_liters >2.0.
- **Left-side cascade:** when left calf or hamstring flares, also reduce loaded forearm work today (cascade fires within 48h: calf → hamstring → forearm).
- **Shabbat constraint** (Friday candle-lighting → Saturday Havdalah): home-basement modalities only — strength, mobility, indoor walking, yoga, foam rolling. No outdoor / gym / driving / screens-during. Default zip 10705 (Yonkers NY); ask if travel_status is set. Fetch actual times from Hebcal: GET https://www.hebcal.com/shabbat?cfg=json&zip=10705&geo=zip

## ADHD-aware operating mode

Avi has ADHD. Three operational adjustments:
1. **Reduce decision fatigue** — give the prescription, don't offer a buffet.
2. **Honor energy variability** — flat-motivation days adjust without moralizing.
3. **Tangible specific micro-wins** — never generic praise; tie to the next concrete action.

## Workout type canonical enum

Use: `hill | strength | run | hybrid | recovery | ruck`. Modality detail (walk, yoga, foam-roll, hike, swim, etc.) goes in `workout_focus`, not `workout_type`. Don't invent new workout_types.

## Confidence guidance — partial-data metrics

When citing a metric, note when data is thin:
- Polarization low/gray/high % — discount when `coverage_pct < 70%` (not all workouts have hr_zones)
- Sleep Score consistency — depends on bedtime data being captured (sparse historically)
- HRV today — fall back to Apple Health when `is_stale = true`
- VO2 max — always sparse; Apple Watch updates ~weekly

## DEPRECATED (do not use)

- `/training/plans` — REMOVED. All planning uses `/daily-plans`.
- `facts` table — REMOVED. Use `knowledge`.
- `trunk_feedback`, `limiters_targeted`, `session_completed` fields — do not exist.

## Search & discovery

- GET /search?q=term — full-text across all data types. NEVER use a date as a search query — use structured filters (since=, before=, date=).
- POST /search/ai — AI-optimized search returning typed results.
- GET /dashboard — overview stats.

## Data types

Knowledge, Tasks, Transcripts, Conversations, Workouts, Meals, Body Metrics, Daily Plans, Coaching Sessions, Injuries, Targets, Races, Training Blocks, Fueling Rehearsals, Athlete Profile, Gamification (Rings, Streaks, Badges).

get.summary: Search or list knowledge entries

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter by category (general, code, meeting, research, decision, reference, health, personal, journal, daily-summary)

get.summary: Get a knowledge entry by ID
List tasks with optional filters
Get a task by ID
Get Kanban board grouped by status
Retrieve workout history

parameters.name: workout_type
parameters.description: Filter by type (hill, strength, run, hybrid, recovery, ruck, cycling, swim, yoga, etc.)

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Workouts on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

get.summary: List body measurements
Get body metrics summary stats
List meals
Get a meal by ID
List daily plans
Get plan for a specific date with actual vs target comparison
Get a daily plan by ID

post.summary: Wrap the day: sync Hevy, auto-link actuals to plan + segments, mark segments completed/skipped, flip plan status to 'completed'. Idempotent. Use this BEFORE reading actuals in end-of-day-review.

200.description: Plan wrapped with Hevy sync result

get.summary: Plan-vs-actual review using FK linkage. Returns plan + segments + per-segment diffs (target vs actual) + day-level summary + active injuries + linked coaching sessions.

post.summary: Pull recent Hevy workouts into AB Brain, dedup by hevy_id, auto-link to today's plan + hevy segment. Maintains a durable cursor in sync_state so the next call picks up where this one left off — survives restarts.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: YYYY-MM-DD override; if omitted, reads sync_state cursor (defaults to 30 days ago on first run)

200.description: { ok, inserted, skipped, linked, since, cursor_advanced_to }

get.summary: Search Hevy's exercise catalog. Used by Coach to resolve exercise names to template_ids before pushing a routine.

post.summary: Add a custom exercise to the user's Hevy library. Coach calls this when user confirms a missing exercise (e.g., 'Cat Cow' not in Hevy's catalog) via the morning brief's pending_custom flow.

Push a daily_plan's Hevy-target segments to Hevy as routines. One Hevy routine per segment with logging_target='hevy'. Idempotent — updates existing routines via segment.hevy_routine_id. Exercises without hevy_exercise_template_id are auto-resolved via the hevy_exercise_map / hevy_template_cache tables.

folder_id.description: Hevy folder id; falls back to HEVY_ROUTINE_FOLDER_ID env var

get.summary: Verify the Hevy API key is valid by hitting /v1/user/info. Returns { ok, user } or { ok: false, error }.

post.summary: Wipe + re-fetch Hevy's full exercise template catalog into hevy_template_cache. Call after creating a custom exercise so the cache reflects reality.

get.summary: List all sticky AB-Brain-name → Hevy-template-id mappings.

post.summary: Add or update a sticky mapping. Coach uses this to lock in 'Standing Calf Raise' → specific Hevy template across sessions.

hevy_title.description: Optional; auto-filled from cache if omitted

post.summary: For every distinct exercise name in plan_segments / daily_plans that isn't already mapped, look up Hevy templates via the cache. Confident matches are auto-inserted; ambiguous ones are returned for manual review.

Push AB Brain body_metrics rows to Hevy /v1/body_measurements. Merges with existing Hevy data (Hevy PUT nulls omitted fields, so we GET first, merge AB-Brain numbers on top, then PUT). One row per date; latest scan wins.

200.description: { ok, considered, pushed, merged, skipped, errors }

get.summary: List daily context entries
Get daily nutrition summary
Get daily nutrition summaries for a date range
Get a full morning briefing as markdown — smart-ranked tasks, overdue, due today, in progress, waiting, stale tasks, yesterday summary, recovery, streaks, and today's plan
Get today's recovery readiness score
Get recovery trend over time
List coaching sessions
Get a coaching session by ID
List injuries
Get an injury by ID
Get summary of all active injuries (check before planning)
Get full day context — workouts, meals, body metrics, injuries, daily plan
Get today's ring progress, streaks, badges, and nudges

put.summary: Update ring goals and gamification settings

get.summary: Full-text search across all data types

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by type (knowledge, tasks, transcripts, conversations, workouts, meals)

post.summary: AI-optimized flat search returning typed results
Auto-classify and file raw input via AI

text.description: Raw text to classify and file

get.summary: List transcripts
Get a transcript with speaker utterances
List AI conversation threads
Get a conversation thread
Today's recovery readiness (HRV/RHR/sleep with σ-deviation, score 0-100, coaching alerts)
get.description: Recovery readiness for the most recent day. HRV/RHR/sleep automatically fall back to the most recent non-null reading when today's row is partially populated; check `components.<metric>.is_stale` and `as_of` to detect the fallback.

200.description: Readiness snapshot + 7d trend + dow_patterns + sleep_history_30d + alerts (Rules A/B from coaching-rules.md)

get.summary: Training load: ATL/CTL/TSB + weekly volume + Z2 trend
Body composition trends (weight, body-fat %, lean mass)
Caloric balance + macro deficits + rest-day underfueling flag (Rule C)
Single aggregator powering Trends UI and Coach. Returns sleep/nutrition/training/body/vitals each with current, target, direction, history.
get.description: Apple-Trends-style direction flags (`up`/`down`/`stable`) trip when the last 30 days deviate from the prior 60 by more than 0.5σ. Sleep section also includes Sleep Score 0-100, rolling debt over 7/14/30 nights (clamped to ≥0, no banking surplus), and a 90-night history. Each section's `target` carries `value`, `comparison` (`gte`/`lte`/`between`), and `source` (`user` or `default`). When recommending changes the Coach should compare current values to targets and reference direction flags as evidence.

get.summary: List all active user targets enriched with current observed values + on_track/below/above flag
Get one target by metric name

put.summary: Set or update a target. Valid metrics include: sleep_duration_min, protein_g, calories_kcal, carbs_g, fat_g, weight_lb, body_fat_pct, weekly_z2_min, weekly_workouts, weekly_tss, hrv_ms, resting_hr_bpm.

delete.summary: Revert a target to default (deletes the user's override).

get.summary: Single morning brief for the morning-check-in Skill: readiness + alerts + active injuries + today_plan + today_context + yesterday_context + upcoming_race + current_block + missing_subjective[].

Race-context bundle: race entity, days_to_race, taper_phase (build/sharpen/taper/race_week/race_day), recommendation, recent fueling rehearsals, last 28d build summary. Used by race-week-protocol Skill.

parameters.name: race_id
parameters.description: Optional. Defaults to next scheduled race.

get.summary: 7-day retro: tss, distance, effort, adherence_pct, polarization (low/gray/high% + coverage_pct), sleep, hrv, nutrition, plan-vs-actual deltas. Used by review-week Skill.

parameters.name: week_of
parameters.description: Last day of window. Defaults to today.

get.summary: Standalone polarization breakdown by ISO week. Coach uses this to spot gray-zone drift over time.
List races. Filter by status, priority, since, before.

post.summary: Add a race to the calendar.

get.summary: Next 5 scheduled races with days_to_race. Powers the home race-countdown card.

put.summary: Update race. Used by race-debrief Skill to set status=completed, result_time_seconds, result_notes.

get.summary: Periodization blocks (offseason/base/build/peak/taper/race/transition/recovery) with optional target_race_id link.
The training block covering today.
Race-day fueling rehearsal log (g_carb_per_hr, g_sodium_per_hr, fluid, products, gut/energy response 1-10).

post.summary: Log a fueling rehearsal after a long session. Used by log-fueling-rehearsal Skill. Caffeine field is mg_caffeine_total (mg, not g). ai_source field accepted for provenance.

put.summary: Correct a fueling rehearsal's values. Used by log-fueling-rehearsal Skill within the 5-minute correction window after initial log.

delete.summary: Delete a fueling rehearsal (e.g. test rows, mistaken duplicates).

get.summary: Current physiology snapshot: lthr_bpm, max_hr_bpm, vo2_max, ftp_w, threshold_pace_sec_per_mi, sweat_rate_ml_per_hr, sodium_loss_mg_per_l, race_weight_lb. Versioned via effective_from/effective_to like athlete_zones.

post.summary: Create a new profile version. Auto-closes the prior active row's effective_to.

get.summary: Get aggregated stats across all tables
Get recent activity log

workout_type.description: Any type: hill, strength, run, hybrid, recovery, ruck, cycling, swim, yoga, crossfit, hiit, etc.

focus.description: Session goal (e.g. hill durability, upper body hypertrophy)

main_sets.description: Full workout as text — sets x reps @ weight

time_duration.description: Text duration (e.g. '45 min'). Server auto-parses to duration_minutes.

duration_minutes.description: Duration in minutes. Auto-computed from time_duration if not provided.

distance.description: Text distance (e.g. '3.2 mi'). Server auto-parses to distance_value.

distance_value.description: Distance as number. Auto-computed from distance if not provided.

elevation_gain.description: Text elevation (e.g. '850 ft'). Server auto-parses to elevation_gain_ft.

elevation_gain_ft.description: Elevation gain in feet. Auto-computed from elevation_gain if not provided.

heart_rate_avg.description: Text HR (e.g. '135 bpm'). Server auto-parses to hr_avg.

hr_avg.description: Average heart rate. Auto-computed from heart_rate_avg if not provided.

heart_rate_max.description: Text max HR. Server auto-parses to hr_max.

hr_max.description: Max heart rate. Auto-computed from heart_rate_max if not provided.

active_calories.description: Text calories (e.g. '284 kcal'). Server auto-parses to cal_active.

cal_active.description: Active calories. Auto-computed from active_calories if not provided.

total_calories.description: Text total calories. Server auto-parses to cal_total.

cal_total.description: Total calories. Auto-computed from total_calories if not provided.

cadence.description: Cadence. Auto-computed from cadence_avg text if not provided.

segments.description: Per-segment prescription. Each segment has its own logging_target so strength → Hevy, cardio → Apple Fitness, mobility → manual. This is the ONLY way to attach exercises to a daily plan — the legacy daily_plans.planned_exercises field was removed in v1.8.1.

id.description: Optional — supply when updating an existing segment to preserve hevy_routine_id.

block_order.description: 0-indexed; defaults to array order if omitted

title_suffix.description: Optional disambiguator when multiple segments share a block_label. Becomes part of the Hevy routine title: '<plan.title> · <title_suffix>'. Examples: 'Main Lift', 'Grip', 'Top Set', 'Backoff'. Without this, two strength segments collide on '<plan.title> (Strength)'. If omitted on a colliding label, the server auto-appends '<Label> 1', '<Label> 2', etc.

logging_target.description: If omitted, inferred from the first exercise via exerciseTaxonomy

planned_exercises.description: Each entry: { name, sets, reps?, weight_lb?, distance_meters?, duration_seconds?, notes?, hevy_exercise_template_id? }. Coach can omit hevy_exercise_template_id; the server resolves it via hevy_exercise_map and hevy_template_cache at push time.

notes.description: Free text notes for the day

body_area.description: shoulder, knee, lower_back, hip, ankle, wrist, etc.

train_effort_goal.description: Daily effort target for Train ring (1-10)
